name: Brazil (República Federativa do Brasil)
summary: Brazil is a civil-law jurisdiction whose family courts (Varas de Família) operate under the Código Civil arts. 1.630 to 1.638 (poder familiar), Estatuto da Criança e do Adolescente (ECA, Lei n° 8.069/1990), and the Lei n° 12.318/2010 da Alienação Parental — the world's first statutory codification of 'alienação parental' as a juridical concept. The Superior Tribunal de Justiça (STJ) has applied the statute in cases such as REsp 1.629.994/RJ (2017) and REsp 1.722.030/PR (2019) while the Conselho Federal de Psicologia (CFP) Resolução CFP n° 13/2022 has expressed institutional caution against PAS-syndromic framing — placing Brazil in a structurally distinctive position with statutory recognition but professional-regulator critique.

statutory_framework.title: Dispõe sobre a alienação parental e altera o art. 236 da Lei n° 8.069/1990
statutory_framework.relevance: World's first statutory codification of alienação parental as juridical concept. Art. 2° defines alienação parental; art. 4° establishes provisional measures; art. 6° enumerates judicial remedies. Substantively foundational for global PA-recognition statutory framework — heavily cited by PASG-aligned advocates internationally.

statutory_framework.title: Do Poder Familiar
statutory_framework.relevance: Primary statutory basis for parental authority under the 2002 Código Civil (Lei n° 10.406/2002). Establishes joint exercise of poder familiar (art. 1.631) and grounds for suspensão/destituição (arts. 1.637-1.638).

statutory_framework.citation: Estatuto da Criança e do Adolescente (ECA, Lei n° 8.069/1990)
statutory_framework.title: Lei n° 8.069/1990 — Estatuto da Criança e do Adolescente
statutory_framework.relevance: Substantively foundational child-rights framework enacted 13 July 1990 implementing CF/1988 art. 227 and UN CRC. Establishes melhor interesse da criança standard and Conselhos Tutelares structure.

statutory_framework.title: Mecanismos para coibir a violência doméstica e familiar contra a mulher
statutory_framework.relevance: Substantively significant DV-protection framework — invoked in DV-PA-allegation intersection cases where PA allegations are deployed to deflect DV findings.

statutory_framework.title: Estabelece o significado da expressão 'guarda compartilhada' e dispõe sobre sua aplicação
statutory_framework.relevance: Establishes guarda compartilhada (shared custody) as the default post-separation framework — substantively transformative shift from sole-custody default to gender-equal shared-custody framework.

apex_courts.name: Supremo Tribunal Federal (STF)
apex_courts.role: Constitutional review including ADI/ADC/ADPF actions on family-law statutes

apex_courts.name: Superior Tribunal de Justiça (STJ)
apex_courts.role: Apex court for federal-law interpretation including Código Civil family-law provisions; Terceira and Quarta Turmas have jurisdiction over family-law appeals

apex_courts.name: Conselho Nacional de Justiça (CNJ)
apex_courts.role: Judicial-administrative apex body issuing recomendações shaping family-law procedure including 2023-2024 guides on alienação parental adjudication

key_developments.title: Código Civil dos Estados Unidos do Brasil (Lei n° 3.071/1916) — pátrio poder framework
key_developments.description: Código Civil dos Estados Unidos do Brasil promulgated 1 January 1916 (Lei n° 3.071) establishing pátrio poder framework — substantively foundational pre-1988 Brazilian civil-law code drafted by Clóvis Beviláqua.

key_developments.title: Constituição Federal — art. 227 absolute-priority child-rights framework
key_developments.description: CF/1988 promulgated 5 October 1988 establishing art. 227 absolute-priority child-rights framework ('é dever da família, da sociedade e do Estado assegurar à criança, ao adolescente e ao jovem, com absoluta prioridade...'). Substantively foundational post-redemocratização constitutional child-rights framework — globally distinctive constitutional priority register.

key_developments.title: Estatuto da Criança e do Adolescente (ECA, Lei n° 8.069/1990) + UN CRC ratification
key_developments.description: Brazil ratified UN CRC 24 September 1990; ECA enacted 13 July 1990 implementing CF/1988 art. 227. Establishes melhor interesse da criança standard, doutrina da proteção integral, and Conselhos Tutelares structure. Substantively foundational child-rights framework operating alongside Código Civil family-law provisions.

key_developments.title: Código Civil (Lei n° 10.406/2002) — pátrio poder replaced by poder familiar
key_developments.description: Código Civil promulgated 10 January 2002 (Lei n° 10.406) replacing 1916 pátrio poder with poder familiar (joint parental authority) framework. Substantively transformative shift to gender-equal parental-rights framework — substantively significant 21st-century Brazilian family-law reform.

key_developments.title: Lei Maria da Penha (Lei n° 11.340/2006) — DV-protection framework
key_developments.description: Lei n° 11.340/2006 (Lei Maria da Penha) promulgated 7 August 2006 establishing comprehensive DV-protection framework. Substantively foundational gender-violence-protection framework — invoked in subsequent DV-PA-allegation intersection litigation.

key_developments.title: Lei n° 11.698/2008 — guarda compartilhada introduced (non-default)
key_developments.description: Lei n° 11.698/2008 introducing guarda compartilhada (shared custody) as non-default option requiring parental consensus — substantively significant transitional reform preceding the 2014 default-shared-custody framework.

key_developments.title: Lei n° 12.318/2010 — Lei da Alienação Parental enacted
key_developments.description: Lei n° 12.318/2010 (Lei da Alienação Parental) enacted 26 August 2010 — the world's first statutory codification of alienação parental as juridical concept. Substantively foundational for global PA-recognition statutory framework — heavily cited by PASG-aligned advocates internationally as model legislation.

key_developments.title: Lei n° 13.058/2014 — guarda compartilhada as default
key_developments.description: Lei n° 13.058/2014 establishing guarda compartilhada (shared custody) as the default post-separation framework — substantively transformative shift from sole-custody default. Substantively significant gender-equal shared-custody reform operating alongside Lei n° 12.318/2010 alienação parental framework.

key_developments.title: STJ REsp 1.629.994/RJ — alienação parental jurisprudence
key_developments.description: STJ Terceira Turma REsp 1.629.994/RJ (2017) applying Lei n° 12.318/2010 in custody dispute. Establishes precedent for apex-court application of alienação parental statutory framework — substantively significant for global PA-recognition jurisprudential development.

key_developments.title: STJ REsp 1.722.030/PR + STF jurisprudence — apex-court alienação parental refinement
key_developments.description: STJ Quarta Turma REsp 1.722.030/PR (2019) and contemporaneous STJ jurisprudence refining application of Lei n° 12.318/2010 within melhor interesse da criança framework. Substantively significant apex-court jurisprudential refinement balancing alienação parental statutory recognition with DV-intersection considerations — establishing trajectory toward CFP Resolução n° 13/2022 institutional-caution position. Substantively foundational for ongoing tension between juridical recognition and clinical-psychology critique registers.

key_developments.title: CFP Resolução n° 13/2022 — psychologist institutional caution
key_developments.description: Conselho Federal de Psicologia Resolução CFP n° 13/2022 establishing institutional caution against PAS-syndromic framing in custody-evaluation psychological reports — substantively significant professional-regulator divergence from statutory recognition framework. Places Brazil in structurally distinctive position with statutory recognition (Lei 12.318/2010) but professional-regulator critique (CFP Resolução 13/2022).

key_developments.title: CNJ recomendações + STJ jurisprudence — melhor interesse standard refinement
key_developments.description: CNJ recomendações and STJ Terceira/Quarta Turmas continue to develop alienação parental jurisprudence under Lei n° 12.318/2010 + Lei n° 13.058/2014 guarda compartilhada framework with substantive melhor interesse da criança analysis. Subsequent legislative proposals (PL 6.408/2019 et seq.) debating Lei 12.318/2010 reform reflect ongoing institutional tension between statutory recognition and CFP professional-regulator critique.

structural_findings: Brazil operates a structurally distinctive civil-law family-law framework with the world's first statutory codification of alienação parental (Lei n° 12.318/2010) — substantively foundational for global PA-recognition statutory development and heavily cited by PASG-aligned advocates internationally as model legislation.

Institutional position is recognition-statutory but professional-regulator-critique: Lei 12.318/2010 statutory recognition coexists with CFP Resolução n° 13/2022 institutional caution against PAS-syndromic framing — a structurally distinctive position creating ongoing tension between juridical and clinical-psychology registers.

CF/1988 art. 227 absolute-priority child-rights framework operates as constitutional foundation — substantively foundational post-redemocratização constitutional child-rights framework with globally distinctive constitutional priority register.
editorial_notes: Brazil's Lei n° 12.318/2010 is the world's first statutory codification of 'alienação parental' as juridical concept — operationally distinctive from the Anglo-American case-law recognition trajectory and from the European institutional-critique cluster.

CFP Resolução n° 13/2022 represents a structurally significant professional-regulator divergence from the statutory recognition framework — placing Brazil in a position where juridical recognition and clinical-psychology critique operate in parallel rather than convergence.
